specs.key_requirements: Add DocsCheck, DocsCreated, DocsReviewed states to StatePhase enum between ChangeImplementationReviewed and ChangeMergeCreated. DocsCheck routing: if no [sdd.docs] config or no crate match → skip to ChangeMergeCreated; otherwise → DocsCreated. CRR cycle: DocsCreated → DocsReviewed → DocsRevised (same max revisions pattern). Add docs phase to Phase Routing table.

Add [sdd.docs] config section to SddConfig: output_dir (default: 'docs'), targets array. DocsTarget: crate (string), guide (string path), audience (enum: developer/end-user/admin), sections (string array). Presence of [sdd.docs] = enabled. Add sdd-doc-writer and sdd-doc-reviewer agent definitions. All execution mode presets need docs phase action entries.

Add OpenRPC definitions for sdd_workflow_create_change_docs, sdd_workflow_review_change_docs, sdd_workflow_revise_change_docs. DocsCheck decision embedded in sdd_run_change routing — not a separate tool.

Add OpenRPC definitions for sdd_artifact_create_change_docs, sdd_artifact_review_change_docs, sdd_artifact_revise_change_docs. Doc-reviewer agent uses Bash (no Write), so must call CLI artifact command to record verdicts. Same pattern as all other phase artifact tools.
Add create-change-docs, review-change-docs, revise-change-docs to sdd workflow command tree. Add CLI → Logic Mapping rows for new docs workflow commands.
CRR cycle pattern reference: Create → Review (APPROVED/REVIEWED) → Revise → re-review; max revisions, auto-approve on exceed. Artifact writing enforcement pattern. Agent retry mechanism.
Per-spec CRR sub-state machine with max_revisions_per_spec=2 — docs phase borrows this per-target CRR pattern for per-crate doc targets. Revision tracking via task_revisions in STATE.yaml.

Merge phase comes after DocsReviewed APPROVED (or docs-skipped) — state routing only, no merge logic changes. Docs files in output_dir are committed naturally by git auto_commit; no special merge handling needed.
Execution loop handles new docs phase tools. sdd-doc-writer dispatched via subagent:* executor. No structural loop changes needed.
